explicit_text: When I started college, I was thrilled to discover Asian American Studies as a subject. It was amazing to learn that I could fulfill my history requirement by taking Asian American history courses, which I eagerly did. These classes opened my eyes to my own heritage and made history meaningful to me for the first time. The experience hooked me and deeply influenced my understanding of my identity and history.
implicit_text: When I started college, I was thrilled to discover a subject focused on the experiences and histories of people of Asian descent in America. It was amazing to learn that I could fulfill my history requirement by taking courses on the history of Asian Americans, which I eagerly did. These classes opened my eyes to my own heritage and made history meaningful to me for the first time. The experience hooked me and deeply influenced my understanding of my identity and history.

explicit_text: In my Asian American History class, we took a field trip to Angel Island Immigration Station, often called the Ellis Island of the West. I learned it was actually a detention center for Chinese immigrants, where people were held for weeks or even months under harsh conditions. Visiting the station was powerful because my grandparents had come through Angel Island when they immigrated to the United States. Growing up in the San Francisco Bay Area, I had never been there before, and standing in those buildings made me reflect on their difficult journey.
implicit_text: In my Asian American History class, we took a field trip to a historic immigration station in the San Francisco Bay, often called the Ellis Island of the West. I learned it was actually a detention center for Chinese immigrants, where people were held for weeks or even months under harsh conditions. Visiting the station was powerful because my grandparents had come through that place when they immigrated to the United States. Growing up in the Bay Area, I had never been there before, and standing in those buildings made me reflect on their difficult journey.

explicit_text: In class, I learned that Angel Island is often compared to Ellis Island, but their roles were very different. While Ellis Island served as a welcoming immigration center, Angel Island functioned more as a detention center, especially for Chinese immigrants. This contrast helped me understand the harsher realities my ancestors faced compared to other immigrant groups. It was eye-opening to see how immigration experiences varied across places and peoples.
implicit_text: In class, I learned that the immigration station on the West Coast is often compared to a famous immigration center in New York Harbor, but their roles were very different. While the New York site served as a welcoming immigration center, the West Coast location functioned more as a detention center, especially for Chinese immigrants. This contrast helped me understand the harsher realities my ancestors faced compared to other immigrant groups. It was eye-opening to see how immigration experiences varied across places and peoples.

explicit_text: Growing up in the United States, I never learned much about my own history as a Chinese American in school. It wasn't until college that I discovered the imperialistic history of the U.S. and its impact on other countries, which my history teacher briefly touched on. Learning about the mistreatment of immigrants at places like Angel Island made me realize that the U.S. has a history of failing to treat immigrants humanely. Even today, I see similar struggles faced by refugees from Central America, showing that immigration remains a human rights issue here.
implicit_text: Growing up in this country, I never learned much about my own history as a Chinese American in school. It wasn't until college that I discovered the imperialistic history of this nation and its impact on other countries, which my history teacher briefly touched on. Learning about the mistreatment of immigrants at places like the West Coast detention center made me realize that this country has a history of failing to treat immigrants humanely. Even today, I see similar struggles faced by refugees from a region in the Americas south of the U.S., showing that immigration remains a human rights issue here.

explicit_text: I grew up my whole life in the San Francisco Bay Area, with Angel Island just in our backyard. Despite this proximity, I had never visited the Angel Island Immigration Station until my college class took a field trip there. Being in the Bay Area made the experience even more personal, as I connected the place to my family's history and the broader Asian American immigrant experience. It was a profound moment to stand where my grandparents once stood when they first arrived in the U.S.
implicit_text: I grew up my whole life in the region surrounding San Francisco Bay, with the historic immigration station just in our backyard. Despite this proximity, I had never visited that immigration station until my college class took a field trip there. Being in this area made the experience even more personal, as I connected the place to my family's history and the broader Asian American immigrant experience. It was a profound moment to stand where my grandparents once stood when they first arrived in this country.

explicit_text: Through learning about my own family's immigration story, I came to understand that immigration is not just an Asian American issue but a human rights issue affecting many groups, including refugees from Central America. I see parallels between how my ancestors were treated at Angel Island decades ago and how Central American refugees are treated today in the United States. This connection made me realize the ongoing struggles immigrants face and the need for greater empathy and justice.

This connection made me realize the ongoing struggles immigrants face and the need for greater empathy and justice.
